summary: PR #1142 moved management of m2c and asm-differ toward pip-based dependency handling. The stated motivation was to remove the manually maintained m2c subtree and let pip-compile infer dependencies automatically. The PR body notes a limitation: asm-differ still needed an upstream packaging change before it could be invoked as `python -m asm-differ` because its package structure was not correctly configured.
what_changed: Removed the m2c subtree according to the PR body.
Changed project tooling strategy so m2c and asm-differ are managed through pip rather than manual subtree updates.
Relied on pip-compile to infer Python dependencies instead of maintaining them manually.
Documented that asm-differ could not yet be run as `python -m asm-differ` without an upstream package-structure fix.
smart_moves: Replacing a vendored/subtree copy of m2c with pip management reduces manual update burden.
Using pip-compile allows dependency resolution to be handled by Python packaging tooling rather than ad hoc repository maintenance.
The PR explicitly preserved a future escape hatch: if melee-specific m2c patches are needed, the project can fork m2c and point pip at a melee-specific branch.
The author called out the asm-differ packaging limitation rather than hiding it, making the remaining upstream dependency clear.
decomp_lessons: For external decompilation helpers such as m2c and asm-differ, prefer package-manager integration when feasible; it reduces repository maintenance overhead.
Avoid vendoring tool subtrees unless local patches are required.
If local patches become necessary, a fork referenced from pip can be cleaner than restoring a manually updated subtree.
Tooling cleanup PRs can materially improve decomp workflow even when they do not touch game code or matching output.
naming_conventions: Tool names are written as `m2c` and `asm-differ`.
The intended module invocation syntax mentioned in the PR is `python -m asm-differ`.
`pip-compile` is the dependency inference/locking tool named in the PR body.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was present in the provided PR slice.
Indirectly supports matching work by simplifying installation and dependency management for m2c and asm-differ.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
